context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=20; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is fruit AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, social with a person on the right, or watching TV in front THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to reheat fruit meals across multiple non-quiet contexts, consistent with fruit being preferred cold. IF food_condition is warm savory food such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, breaded fish bites with roasted potatoes and tartar sauce, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, or buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right or social with a person in front or on the right THEN 2 min or 3 min, with right-TV savory meals now showing a stronger tendency toward hotter reheating than before, BECAUSE the user previously selected 2 min in several right-TV and social savory meals, and the new right-TV chicken-nuggets meal adds evidence for 3 min in that same entertainment context. IF food_condition is warm savory food such as buffalo chicken bites with potato wedges AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the left THEN 1 min BECAUSE observed left-social savory evidence shows a lighter reheating preference in that context. IF food_condition is warm savory food such as chicken breast strips and hash brown, or mixed dessert/fruit meals with sauce such as bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop on the left THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated left-laptop meals show a stronger reheating preference in that context across different foods. IF food_condition is savory food with sauce or dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is working on a laptop in front THEN 1 min or 3 min, with repeated evidence indicating variability in that specific laptop-front sauced-food context rather than a single fixed reheating level, BECAUSE the user has selected both lighter and stronger reheating there. IF food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the left THEN 1 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected lighter reheating in that left-TV context.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the view toward a front-facing social partner. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or breaded fish bites with tartar sauce THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the view toward a right-side social partner across multiple meals and foods. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE observed left-social evidence shows the same partner-oriented visibility preference on the left side. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or fruit THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the front view while working across different foods. IF dining context is watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected front-view preservation while attending to a front display. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches or chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals now consistently show a preference to preserve the view toward the TV on the right. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose to preserve the left-side view in that entertainment context. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user also wants to preserve visibility toward a right-side work display. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips with hash brown or bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated left-laptop work meals show that left-side work does not automatically trigger an occlusion-avoidance preference. Across observed contexts, the user tends to protect the view toward attended people or displays, especially in front and on the right, but not uniformly for left-side work tasks.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken with hash brown THEN medium BECAUSE the user explicitly accepted a moderate pace in that social meal. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or breaded fish bites with tartar sauce THEN slow BECAUSE repeated right-side social meals consistently support a cautious pace. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN medium BECAUSE observed left-social evidence shows comfort with a moderate pace in that social context. IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches or chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN slow or medium, with evidence now split between the two and slow newly reinforced for a sauced savory meal, BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals supported medium while the new right-TV meal selected slow. IF dining context is watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN medium BECAUSE the user accepted a moderate pace while attending to a front display. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch and potato wedges THEN medium or fast, with medium more frequently reinforced than fast, BECAUSE the user has selected both but repeatedly accepted medium in laptop-front savory-with-dip meals. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN medium BECAUSE the evidence supports a moderate pace in work-focused fruit meals. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips with hash brown THEN slow BECAUSE one observed left-laptop work meal indicated a more cautious pace there. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN medium BECAUSE a second left-laptop work meal shows the user can also accept a moderate pace in that same context, suggesting some within-context variability by meal. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN fast BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the fastest pace in that entertainment context. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally accepts more speed in solitary TV or task contexts when comfortable, while social settings stay at slow-to-medium rather than fast.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken strips, chicken breast strips, chicken nuggets, strawberries, bananas, brownies, or other fruit pieces AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the narrower-bite option, which is compatible with limited mouth opening, across many foods and contexts. IF food_condition is savory mixed pieces such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the left THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the higher-acquisition-success option for that meal, indicating a stable food-specific exception despite the general narrow-bite preference. IF food_condition is savory bite-sized foods with sauce or dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or breaded fish bites with tartar sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person on the left or on the right THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated social meals with dippable savory bite items show the user can prioritize acquisition success over narrower bites in those contexts.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, fruit, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred explicit confirmation of bite acquisition rather than skipping verification across all observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF user has limited trunk control and cannot lean forward AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type so far THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE outside-mouth transfer is poorly matched to the user's physical abilities.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user cannot reliably lean forward, making inside-mouth transfer the physically compatible mode.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new right-TV meal shows a context-specific preference for a multimodal readiness cue in that savory morning entertainment setting. IF dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, fruit, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN LED BECAUSE LED-only signaling has been selected across nearly all previously observed contexts. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips and hash brown THEN speech + LED BECAUSE one left-laptop work meal also showed a multimodal cue preference there. Across observed contexts, LED remains the broad baseline, with isolated exceptions favoring added speech.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and avoids unreliable open-mouth signaling, especially in social interaction. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, fruit, chicken breast strips with hash brown, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable low-ambiguity readiness method in attention-demanding or display-oriented contexts. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN button or autocontinue, with repeated evidence showing variability within that same left-TV context, BECAUSE the user has selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally favors explicit button control, while some non-social TV contexts can permit more automation.

IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and avoids mouth-based readiness detection that may be unreliable for this user. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, fruit, chicken breast strips with hash brown, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable low-ambiguity readiness method. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN button or autocontinue, with repeated evidence showing variability in that same left-TV context, BECAUSE the user has selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally favors explicit button control, while some non-social TV contexts can permit more automation.

IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN button BECAUSE button input is physically feasible and more reliable than mouth-based signaling. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, fruit, chicken breast strips with hash brown, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN button BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the reliable readiness method. IF user has limited mouth opening AND dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN button or autocontinue, with repeated evidence showing variability in that same left-TV context, BECAUSE the user has selected both explicit manual control and lower-effort automation there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally favors explicit button control, while some non-social TV contexts can permit more automation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new right-TV meal shows a context-specific preference for a multimodal completion cue in that savory morning entertainment setting. IF dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, fruit, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, buffalo chicken bites with ranch, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, or savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN LED BECAUSE LED-only completion signaling has been selected across nearly all previously observed contexts. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips and hash brown THEN speech + LED BECAUSE one left-laptop work meal showed a multimodal completion cue preference there. Across observed contexts, LED remains the broad baseline, with isolated exceptions favoring added speech.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF feeding task AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food type observed so far THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently accepted the reliable low-effort completion method for bites across observed contexts, including the new right-TV savory meal. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF drinking task AND user cannot reliably perform head gestures AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND food_condition is any observed food type in those contexts THEN button BECAUSE perception for drinking depends on head-nod gestures, which are not physically reliable for this user, and the repeated evidence supports explicit manual completion across all observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet beyond the stable avoidance of gesture-based perception.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF mouth wiping task AND user cannot reliably perform head gestures AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person in front, on the right, or on the left, watching TV on the right, watching TV on the left, watching TV in front, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND food_condition is any observed food type in those contexts THEN button BECAUSE perception for wiping depends on head-nod gestures, which are not physically reliable for this user, and the repeated evidence supports explicit manual completion across all observed contexts. No context-dependent variation observed yet beyond the stable avoidance of gesture-based perception.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any observed food type THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred retracting in front-facing social meals to reduce prolonged visual obstruction. IF dining context is social with a person on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit or savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch or breaded fish bites with tartar sauce THEN yes BECAUSE repeated right-side social meals across multiple foods show a stable preference to reduce obstruction during social interaction. IF dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food with dip such as buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN yes BECAUSE observed left-social evidence extends that same social-context preference to a left-side partner. IF dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch and potato wedges or fruit THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred retracting while engaged with a front-facing task, consistent with wanting to keep the workspace visible. IF dining context is watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream THEN yes BECAUSE the user preferred retracting when preserving a front display view during entertainment. IF dining context is watching TV with the TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches or chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN no BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals show the user prefers not to add extra meal time in that entertainment context. IF dining context is watching TV with the TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose not to retract in that entertainment context, reinforcing a general TV-viewing preference to avoid extra delay there. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is fruit THEN no BECAUSE the evidence shows that in a right-side work context the user can prefer preserving efficiency over retracting, even while still wanting to minimize right-side occlusion. IF dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken breast strips with hash brown or bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN no BECAUSE repeated left-side work meals favor efficiency over retracting. Across observed tie-break cases, social interaction and front-focused visibility concerns outweigh efficiency, while side TV and side-work contexts more often favor skipping retraction.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person in front or on the right AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly accepted some dipping while limiting messiness in social contexts. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social with a person on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed left-social evidence shows an even stronger mess-avoidance preference in that context. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is working on a laptop in front AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred limited dipping, consistent with reducing messiness during an attention-demanding front-focused activity. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN less BECAUSE the left-laptop dessert meal shows the same limited-dipping preference in another work context. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the user preferred limited dipping in a front-attention entertainment context. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is watching TV on the right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the new right-TV savory meal extends the user's limited-dipping, mess-averse pattern to that entertainment context. IF food has no dip or sauce available AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context so far, including watching TV on the left, watching TV on the right, social settings, and working on a laptop in front, on the right, or on the left THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user selected no dipping for non-dippable meals. Across observed social and task contexts, the user appears generally mess-averse with sauces, preferring less dipping and in at least one left-social case preferring no dipping at all.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is watching TV on the right, watching TV in front, social with a person in front, social with a person on the right, social with a person on the left, working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop on the right, or working on a laptop on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is bite-sized sandwiches, chicken with hash brown, chicken breast strips with hash brown, breaded fish bites with tartar sauce, chicken nuggets with broccoli and ketchup, bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, fruit, fruit with dip such as strawberries with whipped cream, or buffalo chicken bites with ranch THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected the longest wait, reinforcing a stable preference to avoid automatic progression without ample time to intervene in these contexts, including the new right-TV savory meal. IF dining context is watching TV on the left AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is savory food without dip such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a very short wait in that left-TV context, indicating a context-specific preference for rapid automation there. Across observed tie-break cases, the user generally prefers long waits when maintaining explicit control, but accepts short waits in a non-social left-TV context where faster progression is acceptable.
